# Untitled-Maze-Game
A game I made for a school project. Used to have sound, but the links stopped working. Along with all the other projects I made that year, this was made on <https://academy.cs.cmu.edu/>, so it requires [Desktop CMU Graphics](https://academy.cs.cmu.edu/desktop) to run. To play without downloading, use [this link](https://academy.cs.cmu.edu/sharing/coralScorpion3906) for the game or [this link](https://academy.cs.cmu.edu/sharing/redMouse4489) for the editor.

## How to play
- Arrow keys to move
- goal is to reach the green square
- Three lives, touching a red tile loses one life
- Hard mode offers an additional challenge

## Level Editor
In order to make the levels, I created a rudimentary level editor. It isn't the most polished, but it got the job done. 
### Keybinds
| Action | Keybind |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Eraser** | `0` |
| **Wall tile (Black)** | `1` |
| **Lava tile (Red)** | `2` |
| **Goal tile (Green)** | `3` |
| **Print map [^1]** | `SPACE` |
| **Export map [^2]** | `ENTER` |
| **Load map [^3]** | `L` |

[^1]: Prints map as a raw list of values, used for debugging
[^2]: Exports map as a string, used in the actual game as well as loading
[^3]: Loads map from an export string, directly from your clipboard

### How to use
Select a tile type (default is wall) and draw on the canvas by dragging or clicking. Once the map is complete, use `ENTER` to export it. **This will use your current mouse position as the starting location for the player**

There is no system for custom levels, but the levels list in the game code (line 90) can be modified simply by replacing an existing level string, or adding a new one.

### Example levels

``
